steps.title: Find the defining signal
steps.content: The strongest clue is Ulfheim's emphatic, deferential introduction: “the honour of addressing no less a person than the great Sculptor Rubek.” The intensifying language—”honour,” “no less a person than,” “the great”—signals that he's marking Rubek as someone of significant importance.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Ulfheim's formal, reverent phrasing serves to emphasize Rubek's high standing and renowned status.

The other three choices either misidentify the source of information (A), introduce unsupported details (C), or contradict the passage's point (D).

That matches choice B — B is correct.
